name: Nat.Prime
doc: p is prime if p >= 2 and has no divisors other than 1 and p

name: Nat.prime_def_lt_dvd
type: Nat.Prime p ↔ 2 ≤ p ∧ ∀ m, m ∣ p → m = 1 ∨ m = p
doc: Characterization of primality by divisibility

name: Nat.Prime.eq_one_or_self_of_dvd
type: Nat.Prime p → ∀ a, a ∣ p → a = 1 ∨ a = p
doc: A prime has only trivial divisors

name: Nat.infinite_setOf_prime
doc: There are infinitely many primes (Euclid's theorem)

name: Nat.exists_infinite_primes
type: ∀ n, ∃ p, p ≥ n ∧ Nat.Prime p
doc: For every n there is a prime >= n

name: Nat.Prime.dvd_mul
type: Nat.Prime p → (p ∣ m * n ↔ p ∣ m ∨ p ∣ n)
doc: A prime divides a product iff it divides one of the factors

name: Nat.Prime.dvd_of_dvd_pow
type: Nat.Prime p → p ∣ m ^ n → p ∣ m
doc: A prime dividing a power divides the base

name: Nat.Prime.two_le
doc: Every prime is at least 2

name: Nat.Prime.ne_one
doc: A prime is not equal to 1

name: Nat.prime_two
doc: 2 is prime

name: Nat.prime_three
doc: 3 is prime

name: Nat.minFac
doc: The minimal prime factor of n; minFac 1 = 1, minFac 0 = 2

name: Nat.minFac_prime
doc: The minimal factor of n > 1 is prime

name: Nat.minFac_dvd
doc: The minimal factor divides n

name: Nat.factors
doc: Prime factorization of n as a sorted list (with multiplicity)

name: Nat.factors_prime_pow
type: Nat.Prime p → Nat.factors (p ^ n) = List.replicate n p
doc: Factorization of a prime power is a list of copies of p

name: Nat.mem_factors
type: p ∈ Nat.factors n ↔ Nat.Prime p ∧ p ∣ n
doc: Membership in factors list iff prime divisor

name: Nat.factorization
doc: The prime factorization of n as a finitely-supported function (Finsupp)

name: Nat.factorization_prime_pow
type: Nat.Prime p → Nat.factorization (p ^ n) = Finsupp.single p n
doc: Factorization of p^n is a single-entry finsupp

name: Nat.Coprime
doc: Nat.Coprime a b ↔ Nat.gcd a b = 1

name: Nat.Coprime.prime_dvd_of_dvd_mul
type: Nat.Coprime k n → Nat.Prime p → p ∣ k * n → p ∣ n
doc: If gcd(k,n)=1 and p|kn and p prime then p|n

name: Nat.gcd
doc: Greatest common divisor of two natural numbers

name: Nat.lcm
doc: Least common multiple of two natural numbers

name: Nat.primorial
doc: Primorial n = product of all primes <= n

name: Nat.primorial_pos
doc: The primorial is always positive

name: Nat.primeCounting
doc: pi(n): number of primes <= n

name: Nat.primeCounting_add_le
doc: Subadditive bound on the prime counting function

name: ArithmeticFunction
doc: Type of arithmetic functions n -> R with Dirichlet convolution

name: ArithmeticFunction.vonMangoldt
doc: The von Mangoldt function Lambda(n) = log p if n = p^k, else 0

name: ArithmeticFunction.sigma
doc: sigma k n = sum of k-th powers of divisors of n

name: ArithmeticFunction.moebius
doc: The Moebius function mu(n)

name: ArithmeticFunction.pdiv
type: ArithmeticFunction ℕ → ArithmeticFunction ℕ → ArithmeticFunction ℕ
doc: Pointwise division of arithmetic functions

name: Int.ModEq
doc: Int.ModEq n a b ↔ n | a - b (a ≡ b [ZMOD n])

name: ZMod
doc: Integers mod n: ZMod n = ZMod n

name: ZMod.val_natCast
type: ∀ {n : ℕ} (a : ℕ), (ZMod.val (a : ZMod n)) = a % n
doc: The canonical representative of a natural number mod n

name: Finset.sum
doc: Sum of a function over a finite set

name: Finset.prod
doc: Product of a function over a finite set

name: Finset.filter
doc: Filter elements of a finite set by a decidable predicate

name: Finset.range
doc: Finset.range n = {0, 1, ..., n-1}

name: Nat.setOf_prime_and_lt_eq
type: ↔ characterization of {p | Nat.Prime p ∧ p < n}
doc: The set of primes less than n as a decidable finset

name: Nat.Prime.odd_of_ne_two
doc: All primes except 2 are odd

name: Nat.Prime.eq_two_or_odd
doc: A prime is either 2 or odd

name: Nat.Coprime.pow_dvd_of_pow_dvd
type: Nat.Coprime k n → k ^ m ∣ n ^ m → k ∣ n
doc: Coprime base: if k^m | n^m then k | n

name: Nat.Prime.coprime_iff_not_dvd
doc: A prime p is coprime to n iff it does not divide n

name: Finset.card_filter_le
doc: Filtering can only decrease the cardinality of a finset

name: Nat.log
doc: Nat.log b n = floor(log_b n), integer logarithm base b

name: Nat.Prime.factorization_self
type: Nat.Prime p → Nat.factorization p = Finsupp.single p 1
doc: The factorization of a prime p is {p : 1}

name: Nat.sieve_eratosthenes
doc: Sieve of Eratosthenes: filter primes up to n from a candidate set

name: Nat.Prime.infinite_of_sieve
type: related to sieve lower bound arguments for prime gaps
doc: Sieve lower bound: infinitely many primes via sieve density argument

name: Nat.card_primes_le
doc: The count of primes <= n equals primeCounting n

name: Int.emod_emod_of_dvd
type: m ∣ n → a % n % m = a % m
doc: Modular reduction: mod n then mod m equals mod m when m | n
